pattern: Swappable index pairs are edges, so within each connected component any permutation of characters is reachable; sorting each component's letters and placing them back in sorted index order yields the lexicographically smallest string.
hints: Two indices you can swap directly, or chain swaps between, can hold each other's characters in any arrangement.
Group indices into connected components using the swap pairs as edges.
For each component, take its indices in sorted order and its characters in sorted order, then assign the smallest available character to the smallest index.

edge_inputs.description: overlapping pairs merge into one component
chain of swaps connects all indices
no pairs leaves the string unchanged
duplicate letters within a component sort correctly

stress.description: random string with random swap pairs forming several components
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(n))
    pairs = []
    for _ in range(n):
        a = rng.randrange(0, n)
        b = rng.randrange(0, n)
        pairs.append([a, b])
    return [s, pairs]
stress.note: dense pairs over a small alphabet stress component merging and duplicate sorting
